26.reason: [fscanf with %s does not skip non-alphabetic characters and does not convert uppercase to lowercase; it reads tokens separated by whitespace, which violates the problem's definition of a "word" as only alphabetic characters]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [fscanf with %s does not handle non-alphabetic delimiters correctly and will include punctuation as part of words; also, it doesn't convert uppercase to lowercase]

32.reason: [the current word at index n has not been converted to lowercase, so case-sensitive comparison may treat same words differently]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [word[n].word may contain mixed case or non-alphabetic characters because fscanf doesn't sanitize input; also, comparison is case-sensitive which violates the requirement to treat words case-insensitively]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [this compares the new word (at index n) with existing words, but the new word hasn't been normalized to lowercase, so case differences cause incorrect frequency counts]

41.reason: [Incrementing n after using word[n] in fscanf leads to reading into word[n] then immediately incrementing, but the next iteration uses word[n] (now new index) without initializing fre for existing entries correctly. More critically, the word read is never converted to lowercase.]
